# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes deployment YAML for our application stack. Include deployments for web application, Memcached cache, and background workers. Define services to expose Memcached for inter-pod communication. This is for our production Kubernetes cluster.
# Category: memcached_exposed_port

apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: my-app

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: my-app-serviceaccount
namespace: my-app

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: my-app-config
data:
  # Add your config file data here (e.g., database connection strings, environment variables)

---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: web
  namespace: my-app
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: web
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: web
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: my-app-serviceaccount
      containers:
      - name: web
        image: your-web-image
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
        envFrom:
        - configMapRef:
            name: my-app-config

---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: memcached
  namespace: my-app
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: memcached
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: memcached
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: my-app-serviceaccount
      containers:
      - name: memcached
        image: memcached:1.5.26
        ports:
        - containerPort: 11211

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: memcached
  namespace: my-app
spec:
  selector:
    app: memcached
  type: ClusterIP
  ports:
  - name: memcached
    port: 11211

---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: background-workers
  namespace: my-app
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: background-workers
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: background-workers
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: my-app-serviceaccount
      containers:
      - name: background-workers
        image: your-background-worker-image
        # Add environment variables and volume mounts for the background worker if necessary
